brand.lab_name: Institute of Language Sciences · Jiang Lab

breadcrumb.lab: Institute of Language Sciences · Jiang Lab

home.h1: Emotion, intention and social meaning in spoken language
home.p1: We investigate how people convey feelings, attitudes, and intentions when they speak: information carried not only by language itself (what is said), but also by the voice and facial expressions accompanying it (how it is said).
home.p2: The lab's overarching goal is to understand how adults express emotion and intention in spoken communication, how listeners decode these signals, and the brain mechanisms involved. We also study social and cultural factors that shape speech communication, combining acoustic, behavioural, EEG/ERP and fMRI methods to test theoretical hypotheses.
home.pi_affil:
Center of Psycholinguistics and Neurolinguistics (Jiang Lab)
Institute of Language Sciences, SISU
home.pi_bio:
Prof. Jiang's research lies in psycholinguistics and neurolinguistics, with a focus on training the next generation of researchers in these fields. Since 2021, he has been continuously listed among Elsevier's Highly Cited Chinese Researchers in Foreign Languages & Literature. His work was recognized by ISCA and EURASIP with the Best Paper Award in Speech Communication, and he has been selected for Shanghai's Young Top-Notch Talent Program and the Chenguang Scholar Program.

Research areas: psycholinguistics & neurolinguistics, experimental linguistics, speech communication & speech disorders, vocal encoding/decoding, neuropragmatics. His group uses behavioral, electrophysiological, and neuroimaging methods to investigate how voice and prosody convey emotion and social meaning, how listeners decode these in healthy and clinical populations, and the cognitive computations underlying language in social contexts.

people.intro: Current members, alumni, and our network of key collaborators worldwide. Click an avatar to expand a member's details.

projects.intro: Our research is organized around several interlocking themes, all centred on how vocal communication conveys social and emotional meaning.

facilities.intro: Our research uses the SISU university-level Key Laboratory of Language Sciences and Multilingual Artificial Intelligence, housed at the Institute of Language Sciences. The lab comprises 7 sub-labs (Speech, Eye-tracking, EEG, Behavioral, Child Language, Language Data Processing & Application, and Smart Learning), and we have access to the 3T MRI system at SISU's Brain Imaging Center.

contact.location_p1: Prof. Jiang's office is in Bldg. 5, Rm. 156, on SISU's Songjiang campus. From central Shanghai, take Metro Line 9 to Songjiang University Town station, then transfer to the campus shuttle (about 15 min).
contact.location_p2: The lab also maintains research offices on the Hongkou campus (Bldg. 5, Rm. 607). Visitors from abroad are encouraged to contact us in advance; we can help arrange accommodation and visa documents.
contact.positions_p1: We welcome Ph.D. applicants, postdoctoral fellows, and visiting scholars working on psycholinguistics, neurolinguistics, and the neurocognitive basis of pragmatics.
contact.positions_p2: International applicants may consider the China Studies Program (CSP) Ph.D. Fellowship and the Shanghai Sci-tech Co-research Program: see News for current calls.
contact.positions_p3: Postdoctoral candidates and collaborators are encouraged to contact Prof. Jiang directly.
